title: error: close exec fds: open /proc/self/fd: no such file or directory
body:
Error in RHEL8 gating tests. I'm guessing it's a flake but it needs resolution regardless:
```
not ok 50 bud-preserve-subvolumes
# (from function `buildah' in file ./helpers.bash, line 47,
#  in test file ./bud.bats, line 494)
#   `buildah bud --signature-policy ${TESTSDIR}/policy.json -t ${target} ${TESTSDIR}/bud/preserve-volumes' failed
# STEP 1: FROM alpine
# Getting image source signatures
# Copying blob sha256:aad63a9339440e7c3e1fff2b988991b9bfb81280042fa7f39a5e327023056819
# Copying config sha256:a187dde48cd289ac374ad8539930628314bc581a481cdb41409c9289419ddb72
# Writing manifest to image destination
# Storing signatures
# STEP 2: RUN mkdir -p /vol/subvol/subsubvol
# error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c mkdir -p /vol/subvol/subsubvol]: time="2020-04-20T18:46:01Z" level=error msg="container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused \"close exec fds: open /proc/self/fd: no such file or directory\""
# container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "close exec fds: open /proc/self/fd: no such file or directory"
# : exit status 1
# error building at STEP "RUN mkdir -p /vol/subvol/subsubvol": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```
I was not able to reproduce on a test VM.

buildah-1.11.6-7, runc-1.0.0-64.rc10. (MB 6373).

comments.body:
I don't know. I no longer monitor RHEL gating test failures except on special request. There are dozens of gating-test failures since my May 14 comment, but there's no way (that I know of) to search or grep all those logs.

I'm not really fond of closing issues just because "we haven't seen it in a while" but without an actual understanding of the problem, so I'll leave this open but will understand if someone else wishes to close.
Yes, it's still happening. Log is internal though.

Again, I don't know, since I can no longer look at RHEL gating tests (it would be a full-time job). I didn't see this failure while reading test logs for [bz1914884](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1914884), so I'll assume it's gone. At least for that particular RHEL and stream.
